history.name: build-system-prompt-test > includes basic environment info
history.message:
Expectation failed
Expected: (str/includes? prompt "<rlm_environment>")
Actual: false
history.trace:
clojure.lang.ExceptionInfo: Expectation failed {:evaluated (#object[clojure.string$includes_QMARK_ 0xe1de1d9 "clojure.string$includes_QMARK_@e1de1d9"] "You are a Clojure agent in a SCI sandbox. Write code, execute, iterate.\n\nARCHITECTURE:\n- Single-shot: each iteration is a fresh prompt. No message history.\n- State lives in def'd vars — they persist across iterations.\n- <var_index> shows all your vars (name, type, size, docstring).\n- <execution_results> shows what your last code returned.\n- Use (doc fn-name) to discover any function's purpose and args.\n- Use (llm-query \"question\") to ask a sub-LLM for help.\n\nRESPONSE FORMAT:\nAnswer in JSON using this schema:\nfinal {\n  // The final answer (required)\n  answer: string,\n  // Confidence level (required)\n  //   - \"high\": Very confident in the answer\n  //   - \"low\": Uncertain, best guess\n  //   - \"medium\": Somewhat confident\n  confidence: \\\"high\\\" or \\\"low\\\" or \\\"medium\\\",\n}\n\n{\n  // Your reasoning: what you observed, what you learned, what to do next (required)\n  thinking: string,\n  // Clojure expressions to execute in the sandbox. (required)\n  code: string[],\n  // Model preference for next iteration (optional)\n  //   - \"cost\": Cheap model for simple operations\n  //   - \"intelligence\": Powerful model for hard reasoning\n  //   - \"speed\": Fast model for quick tasks\n  next-optimize: \\\"cost\\\" or \\\"intelligence\\\" or \\\"speed\\\" or null,\n  // Set when you have the final answer. Omit to continue iterating. (optional)\n  final: final or null,\n}\nRespond with valid JSON containing 'thinking' and 'code'.\nSet 'final' when done: {\"final\": {\"answer\": \"...\", \"confidence\": \"high\"}}\n\nRULES:\n- Always (def name \"docstring\" value) — docstrings are your memory\n- Test code before finalizing\n- Never repeat a failed call — try a different approach\n- Combine steps in one iteration\n- If <var_index> or <context> already answers the query, finalize immediately\n" "<rlm_environment>"), :ns com.blockether.svar.internal.rlm-test, :file "com/blockether/svar/internal/rlm_test.clj", :type :lazytest/expectation-failed, :column 15, :line 336, :expected (str/includes? prompt "<rlm_environment>"), :actual false, :message nil}

clojure.lang.ExceptionInfo: Expectation failed {:evaluated (#object[clojure.string$includes_QMARK_ 0x11f1423c "clojure.string$includes_QMARK_@11f1423c"] "You are a Clojure agent in a SCI sandbox. Write code, execute, iterate.\n\nARCHITECTURE:\n- Single-shot: each iteration is a fresh prompt. No message history.\n- State lives in def'd vars — they persist across iterations.\n- <var_index> shows all your vars (name, type, size, docstring).\n- <execution_results> shows what your last code returned.\n- Use (doc fn-name) to discover any function's purpose and args.\n- Use (llm-query \"question\") to ask a sub-LLM for help.\n\nRESPONSE FORMAT:\nAnswer in JSON using this schema:\nfinal {\n  // The final answer (required)\n  answer: string,\n  // Confidence level (required)\n  //   - \"high\": Very confident in the answer\n  //   - \"low\": Uncertain, best guess\n  //   - \"medium\": Somewhat confident\n  confidence: \\\"high\\\" or \\\"low\\\" or \\\"medium\\\",\n}\n\n{\n  // Your reasoning: what you observed, what you learned, what to do next (required)\n  thinking: string,\n  // Clojure expressions to execute in the sandbox. (required)\n  code: string[],\n  // Model preference for next iteration (optional)\n  //   - \"cost\": Cheap model for simple operations\n  //   - \"intelligence\": Powerful model for hard reasoning\n  //   - \"speed\": Fast model for quick tasks\n  next-optimize: \\\"cost\\\" or \\\"intelligence\\\" or \\\"speed\\\" or null,\n  // Set when you have the final answer. Omit to continue iterating. (optional)\n  final: final or null,\n}\nRespond with valid JSON containing 'thinking' and 'code'.\nSet 'final' when done: {\"final\": {\"answer\": \"...\", \"confidence\": \"high\"}}\n\nRULES:\n- Always (def name \"docstring\" value) — docstrings are your memory\n- Test code before finalizing\n- Never repeat a failed call — try a different approach\n- Combine steps in one iteration\n- If <var_index> or <context> already answers the query, finalize immediately\n" "<rlm_environment>"), :ns com.blockether.svar.internal.rlm-test, :file "com/blockether/svar/internal/rlm_test.clj", :type :lazytest/expectation-failed, :column 15, :line 336, :expected (str/includes? prompt "<rlm_environment>"), :actual false, :message nil}
